questions.question: What is the main drawback of focusing too much on 'Timely' content?

options.text: It is too expensive to produce
It has a short 'half-life' and stops generating value shortly after publication
Search engines refuse to index it
It is too technical for most readers

questions.explanation: Timely content (news, trends) is relevant today but dead tomorrow. It forces you to stay on a content hamster wheel to maintain visibility.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of 'Evergreen' content?

options.text: A live-tweet thread of an industry conference
A 'Hot Take' on this morning's tech news
A comprehensive guide on 'Handling Customer Objections'
A post wishing everyone a Happy New Year

questions.explanation: Fundamental problems (like handling objections) are evergreen. A guide on this topic will be as useful a year from now as it is today.

questions.question: What is the recommended content mix for a founder in the 'Pre-Traction (Zero-to-Ten)' stage?

options.text: 50% Evergreen, 50% Timely
20% Evergreen, 80% Timely
100% Evergreen
100% Timely

questions.explanation: In the early stages, your time is extremely limited. You must focus 100% of your content effort on assets that will build a long-term foundation and continue to attract leads while you're busy building.

questions.question: How can you reframe a 'Timely' news reaction into 'Evergreen' content?

options.text: By adding more images
By focusing on the underlying framework or principle the news represents
By posting it on a different platform
You cannot; news can only ever be timely

questions.explanation: Instead of just reacting to news, explain how that news changes the fundamental 'How-To' of your niche. This turns a temporary thought into a lasting educational asset.

questions.question: What is the 'Compound Effect' of content marketing?

options.text: The way multiple people editing a post makes it better
The way a library of evergreen assets continues to generate traffic over time without new effort
The way social media algorithms punish consistent posters
The increase in ad costs as you get more traffic

questions.explanation: Every evergreen post is a 'worker' that never sleeps. As you build a library, the total traffic from 'old' posts grows, eventually exceeding the traffic from your newest ones.

questions.question: Think about your niche. What is one 'fundamental problem' your ICP always has (Evergreen)? What is one 'trending topic' in your industry right now (Timely)? How could you combine these to create a piece of content that gets immediate attention but remains valuable for a year?
questions.promptForAI: Review if the founder correctly identifies the difference between the two types. Look for a creative 'Reframe' where the timely hook leads into an evergreen lesson.
